ocr.text: The Content Library Valuation for Sony Group's Media & Entertainment division from 2019 to 2024 reveals significant trends
in units sold, revenue, and headcount across the analyzed years. In 2020, units sold reached 150M, generating revenue of
$120M with a headcount of 500. The following year, 2021, saw an increase in units sold to 175M, with revenue climbing to
$150M and headcount rising to 600. By 2022, units sold further increased to 200M, resulting in revenue of $180M and a
headcount of 700. This upward trajectory continued into 2023, where units sold reached 225M, generating $210M in revenue
with a headcount of 750. The forecast for 2024 anticipates units sold reaching 250M, with projected revenue of $250M and an
increased headcount of 800. Cumulatively, the total units sold from 2020 to 2024 amount to 1.1B, contributing to total revenue
The total headcount across these years sums to 800, reflecting the growth in personnel alongside the expansion of the content
library. Additionally, the total content value is estimated at $3.3B, illustrating the substantial worth of the content library over
the specified period. This data underscores the significant growth trajectory of Sony Group's Media & Entertainment division,
